The grade improvement feels hollow when my guilt over this roommate's return keeps growing; I'm clearly trading our friendship for a few extra points.

My message broke the tension, and their casual reply confirms I was right to prioritize our bond over my guilt. This relief proves that addressing the issue directly strengthens our friendship rather than harming it.
The progress feels hollow now; I'm still sacrificing our bond for grades even after resolving the tension, proving my guilt is deeper than I thought.
That tiny 0.02 progress spike feels insignificant compared to the lingering guilt; I'm still quietly sacrificing our bond for points even after the tension cleared.
Even after easing the tension, I'm still sacrificing our bond for grades, and the unresolved room issue means my sleep—and thus my ability to truly connect—is suffering again.

Their apology feels like a relief I've been craving, yet my exhaustion still leaves me unable to fully embrace the connection we just repaired.
